news.headline: What We Know About the F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down by Iran
news.summary: An F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down over Iran and an A-10 Warthog crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, officials said on Friday. Two airmen were rescued, and one is unaccounted for.

news.headline: Chinese surveillance tech rolled out in Africa with ZTE, Hikvision and Huawei at the helm
news.summary:
Across major African cities such as Nairobi, Lusaka, and Abuja, governments are using surveillance technologies and credit lines from China to monitor public spaces and curb crime, a new survey shows.

news.headline: A projectile hit near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing a worker.
news.summary: There was no reported increase in radiation levels. A string of recent attacks near the Persian Gulf facility has fueled fears of a nuclear accident.

news.headline: Iran war: Senegal limits foreign visits for ministers as fallout from conflict deepens
news.summary:
The Senegalese government has banned all but essential foreign trips for government ministers as part of cost-saving measures triggered by the energy crisis linked to the Iran war.

news.headline: Shahid Khaqan urges govt to deregulate fuel prices, promote electric vehicles
news.summary:
